Scenario.title: Operation MidnightEclipse Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Healthcare organizations Simple This scenario depicts a targeted attack campaign focusing on exploiting vulnerabilities in internet-facing devices to gain unauthorized access, execute commands, and exfiltrate sensitive patient data.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Espionage or theft of sensitive health information

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network services, Endpoints
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Web, Phishing, Social Engineering, Malware

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 67 Name: String Format Overflow in syslog()
stage.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks GlobalProtect, specifically a command injection flaw, by manipulating input parameters sent over the network to authenticated users.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 9 Name: Buffer Overflow in Local Command-Line Utilities
stage.description: Upon successful exploitation during Initial Access, the attacker executes arbitrary commands on affected endpoints, potentially escalating privileges and moving laterally within the network infrastructure.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 24 Name: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: Sensitive health records and PII are extracted automatically using command execution mechanisms, bypassing standard filters and protocols to transfer data out of the compromised environment.

Scenario.summary: This attack demonstrates a straightforward compromise path targeting vulnerable internet-facing systems in healthcare networks, leading to unauthorized data exfiltration.
